title: Registry audit
text: audit_everything() walks replication registry attempts every table figure listed engine (R Stata exist). meant health check: failures expected studies, data, dependencies change time. Key behaviour: patience (default 20 seconds) — table figure halted limit; audit continues next object. Failures stop run — results collected data frame. Report fields — study, object id, engine, success, elapsed seconds, timed-flag, short error snippet failure. Substantive checks (default substantive = TRUE) — study defines tests/substantive/<step_id>.R, audit compares replicated estimates published benchmarks (see Fearon & Laitin tab_1). Failures appear [substantive] printed summary. can restrict specific DOIs, collection tag, render Quarto report registry repository:
code: library(replicateEverything)  # Point at a local monorepo checkout (optional) options(   replicateEverything.registry_root = "/path/to/replicate_everything/registry",   replicateEverything.study_folders_root = "/path/to/replicate_everything",   replicateEverything.use_sibling_packages = TRUE )  audit <- audit_everything(patience = 20) print(audit) audit <- audit_everything(patience = 30, dois = "10.1257/aer.91.5.1369") audit <- audit_everything(patience = 20, collections = "APSR")  # Quarto report lives in the registry repo (sibling registry/ in a monorepo) quarto::quarto_render(audit_everything_qmd(), execute_params = list(patience = 20)) quarto::quarto_render(   audit_everything_qmd(),   execute_params = list(patience = 20, collections = "APSR") )

title: Registry audit
text: table built saved audit snapshot shipped package (inst/vignette-data/audit_latest.rds). Set REPLICATE_AUDIT_LIVE=true (point local monorepo) intend refresh snapshot.
code: audit <- if (run_live) {   tryCatch({     monorepo <- Sys.getenv("REPLICATE_MONOREPO", unset = "")     if (!nzchar(monorepo)) {       parent <- normalizePath(         file.path(find.package("replicateEverything"), "..", ".."),         mustWork = FALSE       )       if (file.exists(file.path(parent, "registry", "index.csv"))) {         monorepo <- parent       }     }     if (nzchar(monorepo)) {       options(         replicateEverything.registry_root = file.path(monorepo, "registry"),         replicateEverything.study_folders_root = monorepo,         replicateEverything.use_sibling_packages = TRUE       )     }     audit_everything(patience = 20, verbose = FALSE)   }, error = function(e) {     message("Live audit skipped: ", conditionMessage(e))     NULL   }) } else {   NULL }  if (is.null(audit) && nzchar(audit_rds) && file.exists(audit_rds)) {   audit <- readRDS(audit_rds) } if (is.null(audit)) {   stop("No audit results available.") } sm <- audit$summary results <- audit$results

title: Registry audit
text: Common reasons run fails times : Missing study package folder — install study repo locally set replicateEverything.study_folders_root monorepo root. Stata installed — Stata-backed entries fail Stata found; see Stata replications vignette. Network / data — folder-backed studies may need data files downloaded first run. Patience low — slow tables may need higher patience value without indicating true failure. Re-run locally refresh package vignette snapshot: adding updating registry stubs, maintainers typically run [refresh_registry()] index.csv audit stay sync:
code: Sys.setenv(REPLICATE_AUDIT_LIVE = "true") options(   replicateEverything.registry_root = "/path/to/replicate_everything/registry",   replicateEverything.study_folders_root = "/path/to/replicate_everything",   replicateEverything.use_sibling_packages = TRUE ) audit <- audit_everything(patience = 20) saveRDS(audit, "inst/vignette-data/audit_latest.rds")  # Full HTML report: quarto render audit_everything.qmd (in the registry repo) refresh_registry("../registry", audit = TRUE, patience = 20)

title: Meet the functions
text: Start index. load_index() returns registry index.csv data frame (DOI, title, journal, year, authors, study repo). Example output: search_papers() keyword-searches titles authors. Example output: one paper, list_replications() lists every registered table figure, including engine (r / stata) declared. Example output (abbreviated): list_replications(..., grouped = TRUE) returns one entry per logical group. R Stata exist table, default R. Pipeline steps (transforms) listed separately:
code: idx <- load_index() head(idx[, c("doi", "title", "year")]) #>                              doi                                    title year #> 1 10.1177/00491241211036161   Bounding Causes of Effects with Randomized... 2022 #> 2 10.1017/S0003055403000534   Ethnicity, Insurgency, and Civil War        2003 search_papers("insurgency") #>                              doi                                  title #> 1 10.1017/S0003055403000534 Ethnicity, Insurgency, and Civil War list_replications("10.1177/00491241211036161") #> [[1]] #> $id #> [1] "fig_1" #> $type #> [1] "figure" list_replications("10.1257/aer.91.5.1369", grouped = TRUE) list_replications("10.1257/aer.91.5.1369", grouped = TRUE, language = "stata") list_replications("10.1017/s0003055426101749", include = "pipeline")

what: How Stata replications work in R
title: Stata replications
text: replication.yml marks entry engine: stata (.file path). run_replication() detects engine runs Stata batch mode language = "stata" (Stata engine). R locates Stata via find_stata_executable(), writes small runner, invokes Stata batch mode (StataMP-64.exe /e ... Windows). Output returned R object class stata_replication_result — list (minimum) output_path (log image), study_root, replication_id. entry defines format: code/format_stata.R, R reads Stata log builds HTML table Shiny / format = TRUE. returned object Stata dataset inside R; format = FALSE get handle Stata output disk (usually .log file). Use format = TRUE HTML table format: step registered.

text: Computational replication never easier. still bit jungle. Archives ad hoc structure, paths differ, dependencies drift, every new reader re-wrangle collection files download dataverse osf. Plus guarantees: Nobody runs standing check archive still runs today. replicateEverything contributes proposing format makes code quickly accessible runnable. fighting last battle? might wonder (certainly wonder): AI can rewrite analysis paper fly, keep authoritative codebase? answer: code record analytic choices. results disagree, need shared object inspect, fresh vibe-coded reimplementation. case gets stronger, think, models get better improvising. ’s walk main elements plus bells whistles.

title: replicateEverything
text: Tools discover, run, contribute computational replications empirical research papers. replicateEverything connects public replication registry, retrieves replication materials (metadata, processed data, analysis code), reproduces figures tables published studies standardized workflow. package also bundles Shiny demo app browsing studies running replications interactively — try live demo.

title: replicateEverything
text: Discovery — search registry, look papers DOI, inspect available replications One-line replication — run single figure table, reproduce entire paper run_replication(doi, "everything") Registry-backed materials — fetch data code GitHub without manual downloads Folder-backed studies — dedicated study repositories code/, data/, artifacts/ Package-backed studies — standalone R packages linked lightweight registry stubs Artifacts — load, validate, save precomputed outputs (PNG, HTML, RDS) fast display Display pipeline — optional format_* steps turn analysis objects HTML tables ggplot figures Shiny demo — live app; run_shiny_app() locally; save_local_shiny() deploy Shiny Server Contributor tooling — validate register folder- package-backed studies (prepare_folder_paper(), check_folder_replication(), check_package_replication()) Bundled AI skills — markdown workflow guides assistants (ai_skills(), ai_skill())

what: replicateEverything: Reproduce Empirical Research Results — replicateEverything-package
title: replicateEverything: Reproduce Empirical Research Results — replicateEverything-package
text: replicateEverything package provides tools discovering executing computational replications empirical research papers. connects public replication registry containing metadata, replication scripts, processed datasets required reproduce figures tables published studies.

title: replicateEverything: Reproduce Empirical Research Results — replicateEverything-package
text: typical workflow using package : Browse registry load_index() search_papers(). Inspect available replications using list_replications(). Run single table figure run_replication(doi, "fig_1"). Reproduce results run_replication(doi, "everything"). View replication code get_code(). Launch bundled Shiny demo run_shiny_app(), deploy save_local_shiny(). Contribute folder-backed study build_study_artifacts(), check_folder_replication(), prepare_study_for_registry(), sync_study_to_registry() (maintainer). Audit full registry audit_everything(). See vignette("meet--functions") tour every main function.

title: replicateEverything 0.6.0
text: Unified steps: block replication.yml replaces separate prep: / replications: (legacy blocks still compile automatically). [run_replication()] gains given ("parents", "nothing", character vector assumed-complete steps) force. Default given = "parents" requires immediate parent outputs exist errors missing. Shiny shows faceted pipeline (Tables / figures / standalone paths) hover labels; step labels name outputs. Study products live outputs/ (step-named paths); artifacts/ longer used folder-backed studies. language optional [run_replication()] replication one engine. Shiny: collapsible study details (expand DOI); simplified pipeline key; Pipeline tab per object. Shiny: fixed study deep links (?doi=...) — wait browser URL parsing; skip welcome modal opening shared link. Removed legacy path fallbacks (artifacts/, data/processed/ implicit output locations). Study repos must declare write outputs/ paths explicitly. Format steps type: format children tables/figures; run via format = TRUE unless format = FALSE. [configure_local_monorepo()] wires registry + sibling study folders local dev. [study_output_dir()] preferred name display output directory; [study_artifact_dir()] now internal. Step inheritance — extension studies declare paper.extends inherit: steps; inherited pipeline steps run base repo, extension analyses read base outputs/. New vignette: vignette("reanalysis-studies"). Worked example: Fearon & Laitin reanalysis (rep-10.1017-S0003055403000534--alt-1). Studies without article DOI may use paper.study_handle (registry handle) instead paper.doi. paper.article_url — optional publisher landing page https://doi.org/... fails; [paper_article_url()] Shiny bibliography links use . Registry index.csv carries article_url set stub. [list_replications()] gains grouped, include ("display", "pipeline", ""), consolidating [list_replication_groups()] [list_prep_steps()] (deprecated). New overview vignette: vignette("-replicateEverything") (first article site). [list_replications()] gains compact print method (replication_list class). given defaults "nothing" = "everything". [audit_everything()] runs published-value checks tests/substantive/<step_id>.R present (substantive = TRUE default). New helper: [check_glm_table_benchmark()] logit tables. Filter audits collections = (e.g. "APSR") dois =. Live replication Shiny Run now execute missing upstream DAG steps (parents:) tables figures; Shiny loads merged study metadata pipeline graphs handle-registry entries. Output convention: transform steps write flat outputs/<step_id>.<ext> (e.g. outputs/analysis_data.rds); data steps appear Shiny sidebar Display/Run head() kable preview; pipeline labels add (R) / (Stata) table label appears twice. Registry handoff: [prepare_study_for_registry()] (contributor) validates folder- package-backed study writes short yaml registry/ inst/registry/. [sync_study_to_registry()] [refresh_registry()] (maintainer) install stubs, rebuild index.csv, optionally rerun [audit_everything()]. prepare_folder_paper() sync_folder_paper() deprecated aliases. New skill: include_study_in_registry.md.

title: replicateEverything 0.5.1
text: Registry index.csv supports collections, maintainer_name, maintainer_email, precompiled languages Studies tab fetch study repo load. Shiny study selector uses bibliographic labels (Acemoglu et al (2001)) sorted first author year. Studies tab: collection tags column (APSR, PED, WB, IPI; max three per row) legend; maintainer link study details ([maintainer] hover). Registry study stubs (studies/*.yml) now carry maintainer, collections, languages; [build_registry_index()] compiles index.csv stubs alone. Button renamed Check system compatibility.

title: replicateEverything 0.5.1
text: Shiny footer shows package app commit SHA (pkg / app) instead library install path, easy see deployed app.R stale relative installed package. save_local_shiny() writes BUNDLE_SHA deploy directory; warning banner appears app pkg SHAs differ. Fixed Shiny footer crash (.call(tag, ...) — second argument must list) malformed tag construction app_build_footer_ui(). stata_packages: — auto install probe SSC (including reghdfe / GitHub conflict handling). Custom stata_dependencies: / stata_deps_probe: .files optional rare cases . Shiny dependency-error UI longer calls internal replication_error_message() global function.

title: replicateEverything 0.5.1
text: [package_build_info()] — version plus GitHub RemoteSha bundled BUNDLE_SHA.

title: replicateEverything 0.5.0
text: Exported maintainer API: [check_study_compatibility()], [install_study_dependencies()] (folder + package registry studies), [install_registry_dependencies()], [replication_kind()], [study_artifact_dir()], [maintainer_dependency_hint()].

title: replicateEverything 0.5.0
text: Stata dependency checks study-declared via stata_deps_probe: (check-.study repo) stata_packages: (generic -probe). package longer hardcodes ftools/reghdfe/estout. Live Run probes ; install_stata_deps.runs options(replicateEverything.install_stata_deps = TRUE) (e.g. build_study_artifacts(install_deps = TRUE)). Stata dependency probe study install_stata_deps.load tests use help reghdfe instead bare reghdfe applicable (study probe script). Invoking reghdfe data returns r(301) even installed. satisfied (typical dev machine), install script skipped Shiny shows “Stata dependencies OK — skipped install”. Install runs probe fails. Progress lines reported via replicate_progress() / options(replicateEverything.progress) Shiny’s “Working:” banner. Stata batch runs honour timeout suggested processx package installed; overdue runs killed Shiny can recover instead freezing indefinitely. Tune options(replicateEverything.stata_timeout), stata_deps_probe_timeout (default 120s), stata_deps_install_timeout (default 600s). Set options(replicateEverything.install_stata_deps = FALSE) skip study Stata installs entirely. Shiny artifact loading longer passes install_deps = TRUE (live Run installs dependencies). Study cache downloads unzip filesystem cache directory (system /tmp), moving extracted repo cache atomic Linux servers /tmp Samba/NFS cache different mounts. cross-device file.rename fallback also uses single recursive copy suppresses EXDEV warning. Study Stata dependency scripts (install_stata_deps.etc.) now run per study per session instead every prep step table, repeated live runs longer re-trigger slow SSC reinstall/recompile. missing-dependency retry still forces re-run. Set options(replicateEverything.install_stata_deps = FALSE) skip study dependency installation entirely manage Stata packages . Cached GitHub study checkouts now refresh remote commit changes. materialize_folder_study_from_github() records downloaded commit SHA compares current remote SHA (via GitHub API); stale cache (e.g. one built new data files committed) re-downloaded automatically. remote SHA determined (offline rate-limited) existing cache kept. fixes live Stata/Python runs failing “file found” data exists repo missing --date server cache. remote check -demand (study actually run/fetched GitHub) skipped short, per-session window study confirmed fresh, repeated resolutions within one run make one API call per study; tune options(replicateEverything.study_cache_ttl = <seconds>) (default 300; 0 always check). Python replications now run resolved study folder (local sibling materialized GitHub clone) instead falling back R working directory. Shiny server fixes FileNotFoundError REPLICATE_STUDY_ROOT pointed app directory (e.g. ShinyApps/replicate/data/raw/...) rather study repo clone. Python process now also runs working directory set study root, matching Stata. Python dependency probing uses importlib.util.find_spec per package, prefers Windows py -0p launcher installs Store stubs, skips WindowsApps aliases PATH. Compatibility UI shows full Python path probed. ai_skills() longer lists README.md skill; inst/ai/skills/ now bundles folder_replication APSR_to_replicateEverything.

title: replicateEverything 0.5.0
text: Removed deprecated replicate_paper() create_replication_template(). Use run_replication(doi, "everything") folder/package replication checklists instead. Pre-built vignette HTML shipped inst/doc/ installs skip vignette builds still include articles.

Load, validate, save precomputed artifacts (load_artifact(), save_artifact(), validate_artifact()). Optional display pipeline via registered format_* functions (format_for_display(), render_for_display()). Package-backed replications: install call standalone study packages registry local monorepo.
